name: Badge
description: A small visual label used to annotate or call out a status, count, or category. For interactive states or filtering, use Tag. For user-generated labels, use Chip.

tone.description: Semantic meaning of the badge, drives color treatment.

guidance.neutral: Default for non-semantic labels (categories, counts, generic metadata).
guidance.success: Positive states: completed, verified, active, paid.
guidance.warning: Caution states: pending, needs review, attention required.
guidance.danger: Critical states: failed, expired, blocked, error.

size.description: Physical size. Use sm in dense contexts like tables or cards.

children.description: Badge label. Keep under 2 words when possible.

requirements: Color alone must not convey meaning — pair with text label
If badge communicates dynamic state changes, wrap in aria-live region in parent

do: Use tone that matches the semantic state (success for completed, not info)
Pair color with text — never rely on color alone
dont: Don't use as interactive button — use Tag instead
Don't use for long text or sentences
Don't combine multiple tones in one group without clear reason

antiPatterns.why: Badge is a passive label, not an action trigger. Clickable semantics require a Button.
